item.title: [Comparison of the Consistency and Application Value of Transabdominal Intestinal Ultrasound and Computed Tomography Enterography in the Diagnosis of Inflammatory Bowel Disease Complicatedwith Intestinal Stenosis].
item.description: Zhongguo yi xue ke xue yuan xue bao. Acta Academiae Medicinae Sinicae - Objective To explore the consistency and clinical application value of transabdominal intestinal ultrasound (IUS) and computed tomography enterography (CTE) in the diagnosis of inflammatory bowel d...

item.title: Re: Palmela et al. Use of Intestinal Ultrasound in a Tight Monitoring Approach in Crohn's Disease: A Multicentre Prospective Study.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Letter -

item.title: Utilizing intestinal ultrasound to assess treatment response in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Intestinal research - Review - Intestinal ultrasound (IUS) enables frequent, noninvasive assessment of inflammatory activity in ulcerative colitis and can support treat-to-target decisions without bowel preparation. Evidence fro...

item.title: Ultrasound criteria for transmural healing and response in Crohn's disease: a systematic review of definitions and thresholds.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Transmural healing (TMH) indicates resolution of inflammation in all bowel wall layers and is an emerging therapeutic target in Crohn's disease (CD). Standardized sonographic criteria for TMH and e...

item.title: Nitroreductase-Activated Fluorescent Probes: From Molecular Design to Disease Diagnosis.
item.description: ChemMedChem - Review - Nitroreductases (NTRs), a class of flavin-dependent oxidoreductases, are highly upregulated in hypoxic microenvironments and are closely associated with cancer, bacterial infections, and other hypo...

item.title: Imaging endpoints in inflammatory bowel disease: current evidence and future directions for ultrasound and MRI.
item.description: BMC medicine - Review - Imaging has become a central and rapidly evolving domain in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) because it captures disease manifestations that extend beyond the mucosa and, therefore, beyond what can...

item.title: Diagnostic performance of ultrasonography in Crohn's disease: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Medical ultrasonography - Review - To determine whether ultrasonography (US) provide similar accuracy as ileocolonoscopy, magnetic resonance enterography (MRE), and computed tomography enterography (CTE) in Crohn's disease (CD) diag...
